summary: Frontend testing completed for Training Graph feature. All 7 features verified working correctly: 1) Graph appears and updates after each epoch, 2) Loss curve (red solid) visible, 3) Val Loss curve (orange dashed) visible, 4) Accuracy curve (purple solid) visible, 5) Val Accuracy curve (cyan dashed) visible, 6) Epoch counter increments smoothly during training (1/10 to 10/10), 7) Training completes successfully with final metrics displayed.

issues: Minor: Recharts warning 'The width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0' appears during initial training phase, but graph renders correctly after first epoch

passed_tests: Test 1: Graph appears after first epoch - PASSED (graphVisible: true, lineCount: 4 at Epoch 2/10)
Test 2: Loss curve (red solid) visible - PASSED (Line component with dataKey='loss' stroke='#ef4444')
Test 3: Val Loss curve (orange dashed) visible - PASSED (Line component with dataKey='valLoss' stroke='#f97316' strokeDasharray='5 5')
Test 4: Accuracy curve (purple solid) visible - PASSED (Line component with dataKey='accuracy' stroke='#8b5cf6')
Test 5: Val Accuracy curve (cyan dashed) visible - PASSED (Line component with dataKey='valAccuracy' stroke='#06b6d4' strokeDasharray='5 5')
Test 6: Epoch counter increments smoothly - PASSED (observed Epoch 2/10, 5/10, 9/10 during training)
Test 7: Training completes successfully - PASSED (Final Loss: 0.3600, Accuracy: 87.5%)
Test 8: Graph updates in real-time - PASSED (graph visible with 4 lines at each epoch check)
Test 9: Legend shows all 4 curves - PASSED (legendText: ['Acc', 'Loss', 'Val Acc', 'Val Loss'])
critical_code_review_comments: tensorflowModel.js line 355: yieldEvery: 'batch' correctly configured to allow UI updates during training
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 2532-2576: All 4 Line components correctly configured with proper colors and dataKeys
TrainingPanel.jsx lines 1110-1116: onEpochEnd callback correctly updates trainingHistory state with loss, accuracy, valLoss, valAccuracy
TrainingPanel.jsx line 1094: onEpochBegin callback correctly updates currentEpoch state
Minor: Recharts ResponsiveContainer may have initial dimension issues during training start, but resolves after first epoch
context_for_next_testing_agent: Training graph feature verified working. The implementation uses yieldEvery: 'batch' in tensorflowModel.js to allow UI updates during TensorFlow.js training. Graph shows all 4 curves (Loss, Val Loss, Accuracy, Val Accuracy) with correct colors. Epoch counter increments correctly during training. Note: WebGL is unavailable in headless browser but TensorFlow.js falls back to CPU backend which works correctly. Minor Recharts dimension warning appears during initial training but doesn't affect functionality.
